task:
/home/user/Documents/aws_arch.drawio already contains 3 labeled shapes ('VPC', 'EC2', 'RDS') with white fill.

Extend the diagram into a 3-tier AWS web application architecture by adding exactly 7 new shapes with these labels: 'CloudFront', 'LoadBalancer', 'Web1', 'Web2', 'App1', 'App2', 'S3Bucket'. Apply fill colors:
- 'Web1' and 'Web2' — fillColor #dae8fc (blue)
- 'App1' and 'App2' — fillColor #d5e8d4 (green)

Draw an arrow from 'LoadBalancer' to 'Web1'. The first page must end up with exactly 10 vertices (the 3 originals plus 7 new ones). Persist the change to /home/user/Documents/aws_arch.drawio.

verification.prompt: Inspect the vertices list. Do the two shapes labeled 'Web1' and 'Web2' both have fillColor #dae8fc in their style string, AND do 'App1' and 'App2' both have fillColor #d5e8d4? Answer yes only if all four conditions are met.
verification.description: Web1/Web2 are blue (#dae8fc) and App1/App2 are green (#d5e8d4)

verification.prompt: Inspect the connections list. Does it include an edge whose source vertex is labeled 'LoadBalancer' and whose target vertex is labeled 'Web1'? (Cross-reference source/target IDs with the vertices.)
verification.description: Connection from LoadBalancer to Web1 exists
